steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side
steps.content: Before the blank is an independent clause: “The extensive impact that polymeric nanoparticles (PNPs) have had on medicine belies their minuscule size.” After the comma comes “the nanoparticles typically ______ from 100 to 300 billionths of a meter” — which could be either an independent clause (with a finite verb) or a participial phrase (with a participle).

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: A comma cannot separate two independent clauses. The participle “ranging” creates a participial phrase — which is not an independent clause — and can correctly be set off by a comma. Finite verbs (range, can range, will range) would create second independent clauses, producing comma splices. That matches choice C — C is correct.
